---
applyTo: "migration-reports/**"
---

# Migration Reports Instructions

When working with files in the `migration-reports/` directory, these are **analysis and validation reports** generated during the migration process.

## Universal Report Structure

ALL reports must follow this consistent structure:

### 1. Metadata Header (YAML-style block)

Every report starts with a clear metadata section:

```markdown
# <Report Type>: <filename>

> **Source**: `oracle-sql/<filename>` → `tsql-output/<filename>`
> **Generated by**: `@<agent-name>`
> **Date**: YYYY-MM-DD
> **Status**: 🟢 PASS / 🟡 PASS WITH WARNINGS / 🔴 FAIL (or complexity rating)
```

### 2. Executive Summary Box

A fenced blockquote with key metrics at a glance — the reader should understand the outcome without reading the full report:

```markdown
> ### Executive Summary
> - **Objects**: 3 procedures, 2 functions, 1 table
> - **Complexity**: 🟡 Moderate — cursor loops and exception handling
> - **Critical Issues**: 2 (empty-string semantics, DECODE NULL)
> - **Warnings**: 5
> - **Action Required**: Fix 2 critical issues before deployment
```

### 3. Table of Contents

For reports longer than 50 lines, include a TOC:

```markdown
## Table of Contents
1. [Object Inventory](#1-object-inventory)
2. [Findings](#2-findings)
3. [Risk Assessment](#3-risk-assessment)
4. [Action Items](#4-action-items)
5. [Appendix](#5-appendix)
```

### 4. Numbered Sections

Use numbered headings (`## 1. Section`, `## 2. Section`) for clear navigation and cross-referencing.

### 5. Findings Format

Every finding (issue, risk, recommendation) must use this consistent format:

```markdown
#### Finding F-001: <Short title>

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Severity** | 🔴 CRITICAL / 🟡 WARNING / 🟢 INFO |
| **Category** | Data Type / Function / Syntax / Semantics / Performance |
| **Location** | Line(s) X–Y |
| **Affects** | <what behavior or data is impacted> |

**Oracle (before):**
​```sql
SELECT DECODE(status, NULL, 'Unknown', status) FROM employees;
​```

**T-SQL (after):**
​```sql
SELECT CASE WHEN status IS NULL THEN 'Unknown' ELSE status END FROM employees;
​```

**Explanation**: Oracle DECODE treats NULL=NULL as true; CASE WHEN x IS NULL is required in T-SQL.
```

### 6. Action Items Section

Every report ends with a prioritized action items table:

```markdown
## Action Items

| # | Priority | Finding | Action | Status |
|---|----------|---------|--------|--------|
| 1 | 🔴 Must Fix | F-001 | Replace CASE x WHEN NULL with CASE WHEN x IS NULL | ⬜ Open |
| 2 | 🟡 Should Fix | F-003 | Add NULLIF wrapper for empty string columns | ⬜ Open |
| 3 | 🟢 Consider | F-005 | Replace cursor with set-based UPDATE | ⬜ Open |
```

## Severity Levels (consistent across all reports)

| Level | Icon | Meaning | Action |
|-------|------|---------|--------|
| CRITICAL | 🔴 | Will cause incorrect results or runtime errors | Must fix before deployment |
| WARNING | 🟡 | May cause subtle behavioral differences | Should fix, review carefully |
| INFO | 🟢 | Minor or cosmetic, no functional impact | Consider fixing |

## Report Types

### Consolidated Migration Reports (`migration-*.md`)

Each Oracle SQL file produces a **single** consolidated migration report built incrementally as phases complete. The evaluator creates the file with a skeleton; subsequent phases replace their placeholder sections using HTML comment markers (`<!-- PHASE:* -->`).

Structure:
1. **Report Header** — source file, date (created by evaluator)
2. **Part 1: Evaluation** — complexity, object inventory, Oracle-specific features, semantic differences, dependencies, risks, recommendations (created by `@oracle-evaluator`)
3. **Part 2: Validation** — structural checks, semantic equivalence, completeness, findings, test scenarios (replaced by `@tsql-validator` at `<!-- PHASE:VALIDATION -->`)
4. **Part 3: Performance Analysis** — query patterns, findings, index recommendations, optimized code, configuration, testing (replaced by `@performance-analyzer` at `<!-- PHASE:PERFORMANCE -->`)
5. **Part 4: Power BI M-Language** — object inventory, conversion decisions, folding analysis, connection requirements, skipped objects, findings (replaced by `@m-language-converter` at `<!-- PHASE:M-LANGUAGE -->`)
6. **Consolidated Action Items** — merged and deduplicated from all phases, sorted by severity (replaced by orchestrator at `<!-- PHASE:ACTIONS -->`)

Section numbering uses hierarchical format: Part 1 subsections are `### 1.1`, `### 1.2`, etc.; Part 2 uses `### 2.1`, `### 2.2`, etc.

Placeholder markers enable **idempotent updates** — re-running any phase replaces only its own section without affecting other phases or duplicating content.

### Summary Reports (`*-summary.md`)

Generated after batch operations. These aggregate results **across files** (not across phases for a single file). Sections:
1. Executive Summary (total files, pass/fail distribution, critical findings)
2. File-by-File Results (table with status per file)
3. Common Patterns (issues that appear across multiple files)
4. Cross-File Dependencies (procedure A calls function B — both converted?)
5. Prioritized Roadmap (what to fix first, grouped by impact)
6. Action Items
